assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been recognised as a receivable and creates foreign currency exposure in the consolidated financial statements.
The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for a recognised asset or liability, or a component of such an item, attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk associated with a recognised asset or liability or a highly probable forecast transaction.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
